quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Hungarian language has a special word, 'szívderítő,' which literally means 'heart-warming' or something that makes your heart smile. Hungarians also have over 30 different words to describe different types of rain, showing their deep connection to nature and weather patterns.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, like those you can spot in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, can hold their breath for nearly 30 minutes and dive over 1,500 feet deep. Discover how these charming marine mammals navigate their underwater world.

medium_read.title: The Joy of Container Gardening for Small Spaces
medium_read.teaser: You don't need acres of land to grow beautiful flowers and fresh herbs. This guide shows how pots and containers can create a thriving garden right on your patio or balcony.

long_read.title: The Ancient Forests of the Olympic Peninsula
long_read.teaser: The temperate rainforests near Port Angeles are among the oldest and most magical ecosystems on Earth, some with trees over 1,000 years old. Explore the remarkable story of how these forests have thrived and what makes them so special.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at the Olympic Peninsula
port_angeles.description: Late June is a wonderful time to spot butterflies around Port Angeles, including the beautiful Western Tiger Swallowtail and Painted Lady. The wildflower meadows along Hurricane Ridge Road are particularly lovely right now, with butterflies dancing among the blooms. A gentle walk or drive up the ridge offers spectacular views and delightful butterfly watching.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute when active.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Absolutely right! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to power those amazing hovering wings.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The actual number is about 1,200 beats per minute — amazingly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are amazing — they beat about 1,200 times per minute, which helps power their incredible hovering flight!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: A hummingbird's heart rate can slow to 250 beats per minute when resting at night, but speeds up dramatically during the day to support their high-energy lifestyle. Their wings beat 50-80 times per second!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' comes from an old word meaning 'to flutter.' Hungarian has many beautiful nature words, reflecting the culture's deep appreciation for the natural world.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is most common in Olympic National Park's temperate rainforest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Sitka Spruce trees dominate the rainforest, with some giants reaching over 300 feet tall and 1,000 years old.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The Sitka Spruce is actually the most common tree in the Olympic rainforest. These magnificent trees can live for 1,000 years and create the magical forest canopy you see around Port Angeles.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Sitka Spruce trees thrive in the misty, wet conditions of the Olympic Peninsula. They're named after Sitka, Alaska, and can grow to enormous sizes in the temperate rainforest climate.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
